text.title: First 5 Paying Customers Checklist Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of First 5 Paying Customers Checklist must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Until 5 distinct customers have a charge that has cleared their bank, the product is unvalidated. Friends + family + own card do not count.
rule.rationale: y-combinator essays / Patrick McKenzie writing on first-customer.

rule.statement: Every customer #1-5 is interviewed within 7 days of charge with the 'why did you actually pay?' question. The answer rewrites the homepage.
rule.rationale: fresh memory is the only signal that matters.

rule.statement: Manual onboarding (concierge, white-glove) until #5. No 'self-serve' rhetoric. Operator must be in the loop.

rationale.source: Paul Graham 'do things that don't scale' (2013)

rule.rationale: Paul Graham 'do things that don't scale' (2013).

example.title: Anchored application
example.code: Trigger: Idea → Validated MVP launch Artefact: structured output meeting the contract in content/02-output-contract.xml Evidence: ≥1 named source per rule (URL, ticket, transcript_id) Decision: downstream consumer can act without re-deriving reasoning
example.note: Demonstrates the closed loop the methodology creates — trigger → artefact → decision — with auditability at each step.

example.title: Un-anchored application
example.code: Trigger: 'we should do this thing' Artefact: prose paragraph, no schema Evidence: 'industry best practice' Decision: ambiguous — every reader interprets differently
example.note: Each ambiguity above is a failure mode catalogued in content/03-failure-modes.xml. The methodology exists to prevent exactly this shape.
